forecasts.question: Will AGI be developed before 1 January 2030?
forecasts.key_change: → Stable; prediction markets steady ~25% (Metaculus/Kalshi); no new capability announcements
forecasts.base_rate: Near-zero historical base rate for transformative tech by fixed date; prediction markets 25-40% range

factors_up.factor: AI acceleration continues with GPT-5/Claude-4 class models
CEO forecasts (Altman, Amodei) targeting late 2026-2027
Prediction market Kalshi pricing at ~40%

factors_down.factor: Definition ambiguity — industry CEOs likely using looser definition than resolution criteria
Metaculus community median (more conservative) still around 25%
Only 3.75 years remain; scaling plateaus possible

key_uncertainties: Definition of AGI in resolution criteria
Whether current scaling trajectories continue
Whether AI lab announcements would meet resolution standard
update_triggers: New frontier model release with dramatic benchmark jumps
Credible lab formally claims AGI
Prediction market sustained move above 35%

forecasts.question: Will economic consultancy lose ≥33% value/jobs by end 2030 due to AI?
forecasts.key_change: → Stable; McKinsey 10% cut and Big Four graduate intake reductions signal trend but ≥33% aggregate by 2030 remains unlikely
forecasts.base_rate: Sector disruptions of ≥33% in 4 years are rare; consulting has survived tech disruptions before

factors_up.factor: McKinsey planning 10% workforce reduction over 18-24 months
Big Four graduate intakes cut 6-30% each
65% of enterprises say traditional consulting losing value

factors_down.factor: Aggregate sector revenue/employment still growing; cuts concentrated at entry level
Consulting firms pivoting to AI-enabled delivery — revenue model adapts
33% threshold very high; requires industry-wide collapse not just rebalancing

key_uncertainties: Whether entry-level cuts cascade to senior roles
Whether AI creates new consulting revenue streams
Attribution standard in resolution (AI must be cited by 2+ credible analyses)
update_triggers: Major consulting firm revenue decline ≥15% in any single year
Sector-wide employment surveys showing sustained 20%+ drop
Credible analysis explicitly attributing large declines to AI

forecasts.question: Will Labour be largest party at next UK General Election?
forecasts.key_change: ↑ Labour polling recovery underway per Feb 2026 data; Reform gap narrows to 7-8pts; but third-place risk persists in MRP projections
forecasts.base_rate: Governing parties polling at 17-19% rarely recover to plurality before next election; historical base rate ~20%

factors_up.factor: Reform gap narrowing from 10pts to 7-8pts as of Feb 2026
3+ years to election; plenty of time for recovery
FPTP system can amplify small vote share swings into seat gains

factors_down.factor: Reform UK at 26-31% vs Labour at 17-19%; structural deficit deep
January MRP projects Reform majority government (335 seats)
Labour lost Gorton & Denton by-election (held 90+ years) in Feb 2026
Two-front pressure from Reform right and Greens left

key_uncertainties: Whether Reform support consolidates or fragments
Scale of tactical voting between Labour/Cons against Reform
Timing and context of next general election
update_triggers: Labour polling above 25% in sustained surveys
Reform polling collapse below 20%
Any major by-election swing significantly toward Labour

forecasts.question: Will AGI be developed before 1 January 2035?
forecasts.key_change: → Stable; Metaculus 50% by 2033 consensus intact; expert forecasts compressing but no new threshold breached
forecasts.base_rate: Prediction markets suggest 50% by 2033; academic surveys more conservative (10% by 2035 in some)

factors_up.factor: Metaculus community 50% median ~2033, within resolution window
AI capability timelines compressing across all forecaster categories
CEO-level forecasts cluster around 2026-2028 (earlier than 2035)

factors_down.factor: Academic AI researcher surveys more conservative (median ~2040-2050)
Definition/resolution standard may exclude some near-AGI systems
Historical precedent: AI winters and scaling plateaus possible

key_uncertainties: Definition of AGI and resolution criteria strictness
Whether current scaling paradigm hits fundamental limits
Whether safety concerns delay deployment even if capability achieved
update_triggers: Metaculus median moves decisively above or below 2033
New expert survey substantially changes consensus
Major capability breakthrough or failure

forecasts.question: Will China initiate a military blockade or invasion of Taiwan before 1 January 2030?
forecasts.key_change: → Stable; Mar 19 US intel report (CNN/Al Jazeera) reaffirms no fixed invasion timeline; PLA purges continue to suppress capability
forecasts.base_rate: Historical: no blockade/invasion in 75 years; base rate ~1-2%/year conditional on no conflict to date

factors_up.factor: China declared recent drills explicit blockade preparation
Xi's stated goal of unification; 2030s capability window approaching
Gray-zone escalation trend continues

factors_down.factor: US intel Mar 19: China has no fixed invasion timeline and not committed to 2027
PLA military purges reducing operational capability and confidence
Economic cost calculation: invasion could derail China's development goals
PLAAF incursions at lowest levels since early 2020s

key_uncertainties: PLA readiness post-purges
US-China deterrence dynamics under current administration
Whether Taiwan makes political moves that trigger Beijing response
update_triggers: Significant PLAAF/PLAN escalation in Taiwan Strait
US intelligence community substantially upgrades threat assessment
Major PRC political or military signalling change

forecasts.question: Will UK annual CPI inflation fall below 2.0% for at least one month before 1 January 2027?
forecasts.key_change: ↑↑ NIESR projects CPI at ~1.2% in April 2026 via base effects; Ofgem cap confirmed 11% lower YoY in April; mechanical downward pressure is very strong
forecasts.base_rate: UK CPI last below 2% in 2021; BoE target 2%; structural tendency to miss on upside

factors_up.factor: NIESR explicitly forecasts below 2% in April 2026 via base effects
Ofgem energy cap April 2026 confirmed 11% lower YoY — mechanically reduces CPI
Sharp April 2025 energy spike now dropping out of YoY comparison

factors_down.factor: BoE Feb 2026 official forecast: 2.1% in Q2 (Q2 average may exceed 2% even if April dips)
Services inflation remains sticky; core CPI still elevated
Budget indirect tax increases could partially offset energy base effects

key_uncertainties: Whether BoE or NIESR is correct on April 2026 CPI level
Whether one-month dip below 2% counts even if volatile
Upside shocks from services/food inflation
update_triggers: February or March 2026 ONS CPI release showing faster-than-expected decline
Bank of England updating its Q2 forecast
Oil/energy price shocks between now and April

forecasts.question: Will Spider-Man: Brand New Day gross over $1.5B worldwide by 31 December 2026?
forecasts.key_change: ↑ Trailer becomes first ever to cross 1 billion views; No Way Home precedent at $1.9B; release July 31 confirmed
forecasts.base_rate: MCU Spider-Man trilogy: $880M (Homecoming), $1.13B (Far From Home), $1.9B (No Way Home); trend strongly up

factors_up.factor: First film trailer in history to reach 1 billion views (now 1.1B)
No Way Home grossed $1.9B — sets strong franchise precedent above $1.5B
Tom Holland returning; multiverse storyline has mass appeal
July 31 release — peak summer blockbuster slot

factors_down.factor: Superhero fatigue risk; MCU box office has declined from 2019 peaks
No Way Home's $1.9B included unprecedented nostalgia multi-generational event
Geopolitical/economic conditions could reduce global BO

key_uncertainties: Whether MCU fatigue significantly impacts opening weekend
Chinese box office access (heavily impacted No Way Home)
Competition from other summer 2026 releases
update_triggers: Official early tracking data (typically 6 weeks pre-release)
Opening weekend actuals
Chinese box office clearance confirmed/denied

forecasts.question: Will OpenAI and Anthropic have a combined ARR exceeding $70B by 31 December 2026?
forecasts.key_change: ↑ Epoch AI analysis: Anthropic on pace to surpass OpenAI at ~$43B ARR each by August 2026; combined trajectory points well above $70B
forecasts.base_rate: Combined $44B in March 2026; need $26B more in 9 months; recent growth has consistently beaten projections

factors_up.factor: Epoch AI projects Anthropic surpassing OpenAI at ~$43B ARR by August 2026
Anthropic growing at ~10x/year; even at 4x would reach $35-40B by Dec
Enterprise AI adoption accelerating; both firms winning large contracts

factors_down.factor: Growth rates inevitably decelerate at large bases
Competition from Google/Microsoft/Meta could suppress growth
Anthropic 2026 target ($20-26B) may indicate management expects deceleration

key_uncertainties: Whether Anthropic's internal target ($20-26B) represents floor or ceiling for 2026
OpenAI GPT-5 impact on enterprise adoption
Whether ARR growth rates sustain or mean-revert
update_triggers: Q2 2026 ARR reporting from either company
Major enterprise contract wins or losses
Credible reporting from Bloomberg/The Information on latest ARR figures

forecasts.question: Will UK average annual real GDP growth exceed 1.5% across the 2030s (2030-2039)?
forecasts.key_change: ↓ OBR March 2026 EFO downgrades 2026 growth to 1.1% from 1.4%; productivity weakness persists; medium-term trend below threshold
forecasts.base_rate: UK average GDP growth has been ~1.5-1.8%/year in 2010s; post-pandemic productivity disappointing; 2030s decade is 4+ years away

factors_up.factor: AI productivity dividend could materially boost growth in 2030s
OBR 2027-2028 forecasts raised slightly to 1.6%; gradual recovery possible
UK structural reforms and investment could lift trend growth

factors_down.factor: OBR March 2026 cuts 2026 forecast to 1.1%; productivity growth 0.3pp below previous forecast
OBR medium-term: real GDP per person grows only 1.1%/year
UK debt reaching 96% GDP by 2028/29 constrains fiscal space
Post-Brexit trade costs remain structural drag

key_uncertainties: Whether AI productivity gains materialise for UK in 2030s
Global trade and geopolitical environment in 2030s
UK policy decisions over coming years
update_triggers: OBR or independent forecasters substantially revising 2030s outlook
UK productivity data showing sustained improvement
Major structural economic reform announcements
